title: Prawn Laksa
description: My Thermomix take on a Poh-style prawn laksa — a fragrant coconut noodle soup from a homemade paste.
keywords: Poh Ling Yeow inspired Thermomix recipe
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Poh Ling Yeow and the comforting appeal of prawn laksa. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
steps: Add onion, garlic, ginger, chilli, lemongrass and turmeric. Blend 8 sec / speed 8 to a paste, scrape.
Add oil and fry the paste 5 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add coconut milk, stock and fish sauce. Cook 12 min / 100°C / speed 1. Add the prawns, cook 5 min / 90°C / Reverse / speed 1. Serve over noodles with sprouts, lime and coriander.
tips: Frying the paste is what builds the deep flavour.
Add tofu puffs for the authentic touch.

faq.a: Use 3 tbsp shop laksa paste and skip step 1.

faq.q: Is this the original Poh Ling Yeow recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Poh Ling Yeow and prawn laksa; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
